region_of_origin: Singapore (fusion adaptation of Turkish Iskender Kebab with Indian tandoori paneer)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for Iskender Kebab with Tandoori Paneer specifically; mapped to broader kebab food product class as closest defensible anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP (Kebab) used as broader category; no specific code exists for Iskender Kebab with Tandoori Paneer in FoodEx2 hierarchy.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this specific fusion dish; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Iskender Kebab with Tandoori Paneer is approximately 250 g, comprising grilled tandoori paneer slices over flatbread, topped with a rich tomato butter sauce and a side of yogurt.

nutrient_source.title: Iskender Kebab (Tandoori Paneer) — HPB Singapore Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Iskender Kebab (Tandoori Paneer), cr_id G00010362, with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from paneer and butter composition using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (paneer: ~7g sat fat/100g; butter: ~51g sat fat/100g) proportionally applied to the dish fat content of 26.4g/100g; estimated saturated fat ~10.0g/100g. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and water set to -1 as not reported in HPB source.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; analogous estimate based on paneer-dominant mixed dish with low carbohydrate content
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Iskender Kebab with Tandoori Paneer. GI estimated at 45 (low) based on analogous mixed dish with paneer (low-GI dairy protein, GI ~27 per University of Sydney GI Database) and small flatbread component. Carbohydrate per 100g is 5.24g; per 250g serving carbohydrate = 13.1g. GL = (45 × 13.1) / 100 = 5.9 (low). Estimation is conservative and consistent with the low carbohydrate load of this dish.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 1080 mg/100g — far exceeds the 600 mg/100g upper threshold; a 250g serving delivers approximately 2700 mg sodium, exceeding the WHO daily recommended limit of 2000 mg in a single meal.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this dish; if consumed, reduce portion to ≤75g and request preparation with no added salt and low-sodium tomato sauce to partially mitigate the sodium burden.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate (5.24g/100g) and low GI (~45) produce minimal glycaemic impact; however, high fat content (26.4g/100g, including ~10g saturated fat) contributes to caloric density and may impair insulin sensitivity with regular consumption.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes may consume a moderate portion (150–200g) occasionally, pairing with non-starchy vegetables; choose whole-grain flatbread over refined pita and monitor post-meal blood glucose, as the high fat content slows gastric emptying and may cause delayed glycaemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat estimated at ~10g/100g from paneer and butter, combined with sodium at 1080mg/100g and cholesterol at 85mg/100g, presents a compounded cardiovascular risk profile.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of this dish due to high saturated fat, sodium, and cholesterol; if consumed, restrict to a small portion (≤100g), substitute butter with olive oil, and use low-sodium tomato sauce to reduce atherogenic and hypertensive load.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Saturated fat ~10g/100g exceeds the 5g/100g RA threshold and may upregulate TNF-alpha and IL-6 inflammatory cytokines; sodium at 1080mg/100g worsens RA-related tissue oedema; paprika and tomato in the dish are nightshade-derived.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this dish in its standard preparation; if consumed, substitute butter with olive oil, eliminate added salt, and monitor joint response to nightshade ingredients (tomato sauce, paprika in tandoori masala).

llm_training.natural_language_description: Iskender Kebab with Tandoori Paneer is a Singapore-adapted fusion dish that combines the Turkish Iskender Kebab format — sliced grilled protein over flatbread, drenched in tomato butter sauce — with Indian tandoori paneer as the protein centrepiece. Paneer (fresh Indian cottage cheese) is marinated in a spiced yogurt-based tandoori marinade containing paprika, cumin, coriander, turmeric, and garam masala, then grilled or roasted until lightly charred. The paneer slices are arranged over pieces of pita or flatbread, topped with a rich tomato-butter sauce, and served with a side of plain yogurt. The dish delivers a complex flavour profile: smoky and spiced from the tandoori marinade, tangy from the tomato sauce, and rich and creamy from the butter and paneer. Nutritionally, the dish is high in fat (26.4g/100g), moderate in protein (10.7g/100g), and low in carbohydrate (5.24g/100g), making it calorie-dense at 302 kcal/100g. Sodium is very high at 1080mg/100g, making it unsuitable for individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease without significant modification. Saturated fat is estimated at approximately 10g/100g from the paneer and butter components. The dish is vegetarian and halal-suitable. It is commonly served as a lunch or dinner item in Middle Eastern and fusion restaurants in Singapore. The low carbohydrate content and low GI (~45) make it relatively glycaemia-friendly for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes in moderate portions, though the high fat and sodium content require caution.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010362, confidence 0.95, provenance canonical, review_status reviewed). Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy component data for paneer and butter. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database using paneer and mixed low-carbohydrate dish analogues.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, and water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown and moisture data for this specific Iskender Kebab (Tandoori Paneer) dish are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry (cr_id G00010362) as of 2026-04-16. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, and selenium_mcg set to -1: micronutrient data beyond those reported in the HPB source are not available in HPB Foodo or USDA FoodData Central for this specific dish formulation as of 2026-04-16. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per HPB source (0.0g/100g). Saturated fat of 10.0g/100g is estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy proportional component analysis of paneer (~7g sat fat/100g) and butter (~51g sat fat/100g) relative to the dish fat content of 26.4g/100g; this is an approximation and should be verified against a direct laboratory analysis. The input alias '73. Iskender Kebab (Tandoori Paneer)' contains a menu numbering prefix; this has been preserved in aliases for retrieval purposes but the canonical food_name has been cleaned. This dish is classified as a Singapore-adapted Middle Eastern/South Asian fusion dish served in the local restaurant context.
